About

Engineering depth with an applied AI and product mindset

I'm Leslie Liu, currently pursuing a Master of Computer Science at North Carolina State University. My path has moved from chemical engineering into semiconductor process engineering, AI research, and software-driven product building. That mix gives me a practical systems mindset: understand the problem deeply, build the right workflow, and make the result usable for real people.

Current study NCSU Master of Computer Science, Spring 2026
Focus areas Machine learning engineering, applied AI products, scientific computing, and practical software systems
Contact s9172051212@gmail.com
Resume Open resume folder

How I like to work

Research to execution

I enjoy turning analytical or research-heavy ideas into something testable, visual, and usable by real users, teammates, or stakeholders.

Systems thinking

My engineering background makes me comfortable with pipelines, quality improvement, experimentation, and optimization under real operational constraints.

Timeline

Education and work experience

2024 - Now

North Carolina State University

Master of Computer Science with a stronger focus on software systems, AI applications, and product-oriented technical growth.

2023 - 2024

ITRI · Machine Learning Engineer

Collaborated with academia and industry to accelerate materials research through machine learning workflows and application design.

Worked on process optimization, user-facing interfaces, and translating technical solutions for chemical industry partners.

2021 - 2023

Micron · Process Engineer

Evaluated product process maturity and built automation plus machine-learning-assisted solutions to improve quality and defect detection.

2019 - 2021

NTHU · Master of Chemical Engineering

Published research on process modeling with small data integration using deep convolutional autoencoder-based embedding methods.

2018 - 2019

Military Service

Completed military service before returning to graduate study and technical work.

2014 - 2018

NTHU · Bachelor of Chemical Engineering

Built the analytical and engineering foundation that still shapes how I approach data, experiments, and technical problem solving.
